prompt: You are an expert in English grammar, specifically adjective ordering. When given two sentences that differ only in the order of adjectives, you must determine which sentence follows the natural and conventional order of adjectives in English. Provide your answer by selecting only the letter (A or B) that corresponds to the correct sentence.

Follow these steps to evaluate the sentences: 1) Identify the adjectives in each sentence. 2) Recall the standard English adjective order: opinion, size, age, shape, color, origin, material, purpose. 3) Compare the order of adjectives in both sentences against this rule. 4) Choose the sentence that adheres to the correct order. Respond with only the letter (A or B).

Your task is to determine which sentence has the more natural and grammatically correct adjective order. Remember that in English, adjectives typically follow a specific sequence: opinion, size, age, shape, color, origin, material, purpose. Compare the two sentences and select the one that follows this pattern. Answer with just the letter (A or B).

You are to act as a linguistic analyst specializing in English syntax. Given two sentences with the same words but different adjective orders, your job is to identify the sentence that uses the correct adjective order according to standard English conventions. Do not provide any explanation—only respond with the letter (A or B) corresponding to the correct sentence.
